name: Bitset
desc:
A growable bit array, indexed by [] like a List<bool> but packed
into 32-bit words for compact storage and fast json.encode
round-trips.

name: Bitset.fromWords
desc:
Restore from persisted state. Caller supplies the same
growthStepBits used originally; length is in bits and words
holds the packed 32-bit storage from Bitset.words.

name: Bitset.new
desc:
Create with initialBits of capacity (rounded up to a whole 32-bit
word) and growthStepBits added each time a set exceeds the
current length.

name: growthStepBits
desc: Bits added each time a write exceeds the current capacity.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: length
desc: Current capacity, in bits.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: operator []
desc:
Read bit at index. Throws RangeError if index is negative or
>= length. Out-of-range reads are an error (not silently false)
so callers reason about capacity explicitly.

name: operator []=
desc:
Write value at index. Throws RangeError for negative
indices. Indices >= length auto-grow the bitset by whole
growthStepBits chunks.

name: runtimeType
desc: A representation of the runtime type of the object.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: words
desc:
Snapshot of the underlying 32-bit storage. Word w holds bits
[w*32, w*32+31]; bit b is at mask 1 << (b & 31). Returned
as an unmodifiable view — mutate the bitset through []= instead.

name: ColorTween
desc: A Tween that interpolates between two Colors using Color.lerp.

name: colorFrom
desc:
The colour the tween starts at; reported to onStart and to
onUpdate on the first run-tick.

name: colorTo
desc:
The colour the tween ends at; reported to onUpdate and onComplete
on the final tick.

name: ColorTweenCallback
desc:
Callback for ColorTween lifecycle events. Receives the current
interpolated colour.

name: Config
desc: Runtime configuration loaded from a .jx file.

name: asARGBColor
desc:
Read a numeric value at "section.property" and interpret it as an ARGB
color (alpha in the high byte, 0xAARRGGBB). Returns defaultValue (or
null) if the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError for non-numeric
values.

name: asBool
desc:
Read a bool value at "section.property". Strict: the value must already
be a bool — strings like 'true' are not coerced. Returns defaultValue
(or null) if the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError for any other
type.

name: asColor
desc:
Read a numeric value at "section.property" and interpret it as an RGB
color. The low 24 bits supply red/green/blue and alpha is forced to
fully opaque (0xFF). JX supports RGBA literals but they're not
distinguishable here, so the value is always treated as RGB. Returns
defaultValue (or null) if the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError
for non-numeric values.

name: asDouble
desc:
Read a double value at "section.property". Accepts double as-is and
widens int via toDouble(). Returns defaultValue (or null) if the
key is missing. Throws ArgumentError for non-numeric values.

name: asInt
desc:
Read an int value at "section.property". Accepts int as-is and
truncates double via toInt(). Returns defaultValue (or null) if
the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError for non-numeric values.

name: asOffset
desc:
Read a numeric value at "section.property" and interpret it as an
offset between min and max:

name: asPos
desc:
Read an object value at "section.property" and interpret it as a
position in screen pixels, relative to a window on the current
SizzleGame. The object can carry:

name: asRGBAColor
desc:
Read a numeric value at "section.property" and interpret it as an RGBA
color (alpha in the low byte, 0xRRGGBBAA). The alpha byte is rotated
into the high position to produce a Flutter Color. Returns
defaultValue (or null) if the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError
for non-numeric values.

name: asStr
desc:
Read a string value at "section.property". Returns defaultValue (or
null) if the key is missing. Throws ArgumentError if the value is
present but not a string.

name: capabilities
desc:
The capabilities used to flatten the current snapshot. Assigning a new
value re-flattens in place and fires onChange. Assigning a value that
is equivalent to the current one (treating missing keys as false) is a
no-op.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: load
desc:
Load and parse a JX config file from the asset bundle via
Services.files. The file is not added to the file cache.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: onChange
desc:
Called after the capabilities setter rebuilds the snapshot. Not called
when the new capabilities are equivalent to the old ones (treating any
missing key as false). Assign directly:
config.onChange = () => ...;

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: operator []
desc:
Raw indexed access. Path must be exactly "section.property" — a single
dot separating two non-empty parts. Returns null if the key is missing.
Throws ArgumentError for any other path shape.

name: parse
desc: Parse a JX config from a string. Useful for tests and inline configs.

The section names present in the flattened snapshot, in document order.
Lets callers enumerate an open-ended set of sections (e.g. one section
per catalogue entry) without hard-coding their keys.

The dialogSize dictates the maximum width and height of the
dialog box.

name: absoluteAngle
desc:
The resulting angle after all the ancestors and the components own angles
and scales have been applied.

name: absoluteAngleWithoutReflection
desc:
The resulting angle after all the ancestors and the components own angles
and scales have been applied, but without reflecting the angle
if the component is flipped.

name: absoluteCenter
desc: The absolute center of the component.

name: absolutePosition
desc: The anchor's position in absolute (world) coordinates.

name: absolutePositionOf
desc:
Convert local coordinates of a point point inside the component
into the global (world) coordinate space.

name: absolutePositionOfAnchor
desc:
Similar to absolutePositionOf(), but applies to any anchor
point within the component.

name: absoluteScale
desc:
The resulting scale after all the ancestors and the components own scale
has been applied.

name: absoluteScaledSize
desc:
The resulting size after all the ancestors and the components own scale
has been applied.

name: absoluteToLocal
desc:
Transform point from the global (world) coordinate space into the
local coordinates. This function is the inverse of
absolutePositionOf().

name: absoluteTopLeftPosition
desc: The absolute top left position regardless of whether it is a child or not.

name: add
desc: Schedules component to be added as a child to this component.

name: addAll
desc: A convenience method to add multiple children at once.

name: addToParent
desc: Adds this component as a child of parent (see add for details).

name: afterChildrenRendered
desc: Called once after all children have been rendered in renderTree.

name: ancestors
desc:
An iterator producing this component's parent, then its parent's parent,
then the great-grand-parent, and so on, until it reaches a component
without a parent.

name: anchor
desc:
Anchor point for this component. An anchor point describes a point
within the rectangle of size size. This point is considered to
be the logical "center" of the component. This can be visualized
as the point where Flame "grabs" the component. All transforms
occur around this point: the position is where the anchor point
will end up after the component is translated; the rotation and
scaling also happen around this anchor point.

name: angle
desc:
Rotation angle (in radians) of the component. The component will be
rotated around its anchor point in the clockwise direction if the
angle is positive, or counterclockwise if the angle is negative.

name: angleTo
desc:
Returns the angle formed by component's orientation vector and a vector
starting at component's absolute position and ending at target. I.e.
how much the current component need to rotate to face the target. This
angle is measured in clockwise direction. target should be in
absolute/world coordinate system.

name: center
desc:
The position of the center of the component's bounding rectangle
in the parent's coordinates.

name: children
desc: The children components of this component.

name: componentsAtLocation
desc:
This is a generic implementation of componentsAtPoint; refer to those
docs for context.

name: componentsAtPoint
desc:
An iterable of descendant components intersecting the given point. The
point is in the local coordinate space.

name: contains
desc: Whether the children list contains the given component.

name: containsLocalPoint
desc:
Test whether the point (given in local coordinates) lies within this
component. The top and the left borders of the component are inclusive,
while the bottom and the right borders are exclusive.

name: containsPoint
desc:
Test whether the point (given in global coordinates) lies within this
component. The top and the left borders of the component are inclusive,
while the bottom and the right borders are exclusive.

name: createComponentSet
desc:
This method creates the children container for the current component.
Override this method if you need to have a custom OrderedSet within
a particular class.

name: debugColor
desc: The color that the debug output should be rendered with.

name: debugCoordinatesPrecision
desc:
How many decimal digits to print when displaying coordinates in the
debug mode. Setting this to null will suppress all coordinates from
the output.

name: debugMode
desc:
Returns whether this Component is in debug mode or not.
When a child is added to the Component it gets the same debugMode as
its parent has when it is prepared.

name: debugPaint
desc: The debugColor represented as a Paint object.

name: debugTextPaint
desc:
Returns a TextPaint object with the debugColor set as color for the
text.

name: decorator
desc: The decorator is used to apply visual effects to a component.

name: descendants
desc: Recursively enumerates all nested children.

name: dialogueRunner
desc:
The owner of this DialogueView. This property will be null when the
dialogue view hasn't been attached to any DialogueRunner yet.

name: distance
desc:
Measure the distance (in parent's coordinate space) between this
component's anchor and the other component's anchor.

name: findGame
desc: Fetches the nearest FlameGame ancestor to the component.

name: findParent
desc:
Returns the closest parent further up the hierarchy that satisfies type=T,
or null if no such parent can be found.

name: findRootGame
desc: Fetches the root FlameGame ancestor to the component.

name: firstChild
desc:
Returns the first child that matches the given type T, or null if there
are no such children.

name: flipHorizontally
desc: Flip the component horizontally around its anchor point.

name: flipHorizontallyAroundCenter
desc: Flip the component horizontally around its center line.

name: flipVertically
desc: Flip the component vertically around its anchor point.

name: flipVerticallyAroundCenter
desc: Flip the component vertically around its center line.

name: hasChildren
desc:
Whether this component has any children.
Avoids the creation of the children container if not necessary.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: height
desc:
The height of the component in local coordinates. Note that the object
may visually appear larger or smaller due to application of scale.

name: isFlippedHorizontally
desc: Whether it is currently flipped horizontally.

name: isFlippedVertically
desc: Whether it is currently flipped vertically.

name: isHovered
desc: Returns true while the component is being dragged.

name: isLoaded
desc: Whether this component has completed its onLoad step.

name: isLoading
desc: Whether the component is currently executing its onLoad step.

name: isMounted
desc: Whether this component is currently added to a component tree.

name: isRemoved
desc:
Whether the component has been removed. Originally this flag is false,
but it becomes true after the component was mounted and then removed
from its parent. The flag becomes false again when the component is
mounted to a new parent.

name: isRemoving
desc: Whether the component is scheduled to be removed.

name: key
desc: A key that can be used to identify this component in the tree.

name: lastChild
desc:
Returns the last child that matches the given type T, or null if there
are no such children.

name: loaded
desc: A future that completes when this component finishes loading.

name: lookAt
desc: Rotates/snaps the component to look at the target.

name: mounted
desc: A future that will complete once the component is mounted on its parent.

name: nativeAngle
desc:
The angle where this component is looking at when it is in
the default state, i.e. when angle is equal to zero.
For example, a nativeAngle of
    0 implies up/north direction
 pi/2 implies right/east direction
   pi implies down/south direction
-pi/2 implies left/west direction

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: onChildrenChanged
desc:
This method will be invoked from lifecycle if child has been added
to or removed from its parent children list.

name: onChoiceFinish
desc: Called when the choice has been made, and the option was selected.

name: onChoiceStart
desc:
Called when the dialogue arrives at an option set, and the player must now
make a choice on how to proceed. If a dialogue view presents this choice
to the player and allows them to make a selection, then it must return a
future that completes when the choice is made. If the dialogue view does
not display menu choice, then it should return null (possibly in a
Future).

name: onCommand
desc:
Forwarded from Jenny when a yarn command runs. Delegates to the default
DialogueView implementation - see Jenny's documentation for details.

name: onDialogueFinish
desc: Called when the dialogue has ended.

name: onDialogueStart
desc:
Called before the start of a new dialogue, i.e. before any lines, options,
or commands are delivered.

name: onFindCharacterPosition
desc:
Called by the dialog to locate a character in the scene
so that the bubble can be positioned correctly.

name: onFinish
desc: Called by the dialog when a dialog session ends.

name: onGameResize
desc: Called whenever the size of the top-level Canvas changes.

name: onHotReload
desc: Called when Flutter's hot reload is triggered.

name: onLineFinish
desc: Called when the line has finished presenting in all dialogue views.

name: onLineSignal
desc: Called when the dialogue runner sends a signal to all dialogue views.

name: onLineStart
desc: Called when the next dialogue line should be presented to the user.

name: onLineStop
desc:
Called when the game demands that the line finished presenting as soon
as possible.

name: onLoad
desc: Late initialization method for Component.

name: onMount
desc: Called when the component is added to its parent.

name: onNodeFinish
desc:
Forwarded from Jenny when the runner leaves a node. Delegates to the
default DialogueView implementation - see Jenny's documentation for
details.

name: onNodeStart
desc: Called when the dialogue enters a new node.

name: onParentResize
desc:
Called whenever the parent of this component changes size; and also once
before onMount.

name: onRemove
desc:
Called right before the component is removed from its parent
and also before it changes parents (and is thus temporarily removed
from the component tree).

name: onStart
desc: Called by the dialog when a dialog session starts.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: parent
desc: Who owns this component in the component tree.

name: position
desc: The position of this component's anchor on the screen.

name: positionOf
desc:
Convert local coordinates of a point point inside the component
into the parent's coordinate space.

name: positionOfAnchor
desc:
Similar to positionOf(), but applies to any anchor point within
the component.

name: priority
desc:
Render priority of this component. This allows you to control the order in
which your components are rendered.

name: propagateToChildren
desc:
This method first calls the passed handler on the leaves in the tree,
the children without any children of their own.
Then it continues through all other children. The propagation continues
until the handler returns false, which means "do not continue", or when
the handler has been called with all children.

name: remove
desc: Removes a component from the component tree.

name: removeAll
desc:
Removes all the children in the list and calls onRemove for all of them
and their children.

name: removeFromParent
desc: Remove the component from its parent in the next tick.

name: removeWhere
desc: Removes all the children for which the test function returns true.

name: removed
desc: A future that completes when this component is removed from its parent.

name: renderChild
desc: Renders a single child component onto canvas.

name: renderContext
desc:
Override this method if you want your component to provide a custom
render context to all its children (recursively).

name: runtimeType
desc: A representation of the runtime type of the object.

name: scale
desc:
The scale factor of this component. The scale can be different along
the X and Y dimensions. A scale greater than 1 makes the component
bigger, and less than 1 smaller. The scale can also be negative,
which results in a mirror reflection along the corresponding axis.

name: scaledSize
desc:
The "physical" size of the component. This is the size of the
component as seen from the parent's perspective, and it is equal to
size * scale. This is a computed property and cannot be
modified by the user.

name: setByRect
desc:
Mutates position and size using the provided rect as basis.
This is a relative rect, same definition that toRect use
(therefore both methods are compatible,
i.e. setByRect ∘ toRect = identity).

name: size
desc:
The logical size of the component. The game assumes that this is the
approximate size of the object that will be drawn on the screen.
This size will therefore be used for collision detection and tap
handling.

name: toAbsoluteRect
desc: The bounding rectangle of the component in global coordinate space.

name: toLocal
desc:
Transform point from the parent's coordinate space into the local
coordinates. This function is the inverse of positionOf().

name: toRect
desc: Returns the bounding rectangle for this component.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: topLeftPosition
desc: The top-left corner's position in the parent's coordinates.

name: transformMatrix
desc:
The total transformation matrix for the component. This matrix combines
translation, rotation and scale transforms into a single entity. The
matrix is cached and gets recalculated only as necessary.

name: update
desc:
This method is called periodically by the game engine to request that your
component updates itself.

name: updateTree
desc:
This method traverses the component tree and calls update on all its
children according to their priority order, relative to the
priority of the direct siblings, not the children or the ancestors.

name: width
desc:
The width of the component in local coordinates. Note that the object
may visually appear larger or smaller due to application of scale.

name: x
desc: X position of this component's anchor on the screen.

name: y
desc: Y position of this component's anchor on the screen.

name: DirectionalLight
desc:
Parallel-ray light from direction (think sunlight). The vector is
normalised at construction and on assignment, so callers may pass any
non-zero vector. A zero vector is preserved as-is (no NaN).

name: Easing
desc:
Collection of easing functions that map a normalized progress value
p in [0, 1] to an eased value (typically also in [0, 1], though
some functions overshoot).

name: backEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the overshooting cubic y = x^3-xsin(xpi)

name: backEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise overshooting cubic function:
y = (1/2)((2x)^3-(2x)sin(2xpi))           ; [0, 0.5)
y = (1/2)*(1-((1-x)^3-(1-x)*sin((1-x)*pi))+1) ; 0.5, 1

name: backEaseOut
desc: Modeled after overshooting cubic y = 1-((1-x)^3-(1-x)*sin((1-x)*pi))

name: circularEaseIn
desc: Modeled after shifted quadrant IV of unit circle

name: circularEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise circular function
y = (1/2)(1 - sqrt(1 - 4x^2))           ; [0, 0.5)
y = (1/2)(sqrt(-(2x - 3)*(2x - 1)) + 1) ; 0.5, 1

name: circularEaseOut
desc: Modeled after shifted quadrant II of unit circle

name: cubicEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the cubic y = x^3

name: cubicEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise cubic
y = (1/2)((2x)^3)       ; [0, 0.5)
y = (1/2)((2x-2)^3 + 2) ; 0.5, 1

name: cubicEaseOut
desc: Modeled after the cubic y = (x - 1)^3 + 1

name: elasticEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the damped sine wave y = sin(13pi/2*x)*pow(2, 10 * (x - 1))

name: elasticEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise exponentially-damped sine wave:
y = (1/2)sin(13pi/2(2x))pow(2, 10 * ((2x) - 1))      ; [0,0.5)
y = (1/2)(sin(-13pi/2*((2x-1)+1))pow(2,-10(2x-1)) + 2) ; 0.5, 1

name: elasticEaseOut
desc:
Modeled after the damped sine wave
y = sin(-13pi/2*(x + 1))*pow(2, -10x) + 1

name: exponentialEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the exponential function y = 2^(10(x - 1))

name: exponentialEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise exponential
y = (1/2)2^(10(2x - 1))         ; [0,0.5)
y = -(1/2)*2^(-10(2x - 1))) + 1 ; 0.5,1

name: exponentialEaseOut
desc: Modeled after the exponential function y = -2^(-10x) + 1

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: none
desc: Modeled after the line y = x

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: quadraticEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the parabola y = x^2

name: quadraticEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise quadratic
y = (1/2)((2x)^2)             ; [0, 0.5)
y = -(1/2)((2x-1)*(2x-3) - 1) ; 0.5, 1

name: quadraticEaseOut
desc: Modeled after the parabola y = -x^2 + 2x

name: quarticEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the quartic x^4

name: quarticEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise quartic
y = (1/2)((2x)^4)        ; [0, 0.5)
y = -(1/2)((2x-2)^4 - 2) ; 0.5, 1

name: quarticEaseOut
desc: Modeled after the quartic y = 1 - (x - 1)^4

name: quinticEaseIn
desc: Modeled after the quintic y = x^5

name: quinticEaseInOut
desc:
Modeled after the piecewise quintic
y = (1/2)((2x)^5)       ; [0, 0.5)
y = (1/2)((2x-2)^5 + 2) ; 0.5, 1

name: quinticEaseOut
desc: Modeled after the quintic y = (x - 1)^5 + 1

name: sineEaseInOut

name: resolvePathColor
desc:
Resolve the final fill colour for path given its group material, the

environment's lights. Pure function — exposed for testing.

name: shadowFactor
desc:
Sheen-independent shadow strength in single-colour mode. Below the
shading midpoint, surfaces darken at this rate regardless of sheen,
giving uniformly deep shadows where dull/matte/gloss read as similar.

name: shadowSheenWeight
desc:
How much sheen still flavours the shadow side, as a multiplier on
sheenFactor. 0 = shadow depth is purely shadowFactor; 1 = shadow
would scale with sheen as much as the highlight does. Small values
keep gloss slightly deeper than dull while still reading as "dark".

name: sheenFactor
desc:
Per-sheen highlight strength in single-colour mode — controls how much
the surface lightens above the shading midpoint. Specular bypasses this
and modulates alpha instead.

name: snapshotAsImage

Convert the cached lit picture to an Image of the given width and
height (in pixels). Use transform to position the snapshot inside
the image, or to apply other transforms before rasterisation.

name: specularExponent
desc: Specular hardness exponent. Higher = sharper highlight.

name: tintStrength
desc:
How strongly a light's colour pushes the surface toward that colour.
Tinting is multiplicative: c * (1 + tint * tintStrength).

name: LitSvgMaterial
desc:
The material definition for a group, as defined in the pp:material attribute.
The attribute is either 1, 2 or 3 items separated by whitespace:
"baseColor" (topColor = baseColor, sheen = matte)
"baseColor sheen" (topColor = baseColor)
"baseColor topColor sheen"
The baseColor and topColor are 3, 6, or 8 digit hex:
#RGB (each digit doubled, alpha is assumed to be 255)
#RRGGBB (alpha is assumed to be 255)
#AARRGGBB
Sheen may be dull, matte, gloss, specular, or just the first letter
(d, m, g, s).

An iterator producing this component's parent, then its parent's parent,
then the great-grand-parent, and so on, until it reaches a component
without a parent.

name: anchor
desc:
Anchor point for this component. An anchor point describes a point
within the rectangle of size size. This point is considered to
be the logical "center" of the component. This can be visualized
as the point where Flame "grabs" the component. All transforms
occur around this point: the position is where the anchor point
will end up after the component is translated; the rotation and
scaling also happen around this anchor point.

name: angle
desc:
Rotation angle (in radians) of the component. The component will be
rotated around its anchor point in the clockwise direction if the
angle is positive, or counterclockwise if the angle is negative.

name: angleTo
desc:
Returns the angle formed by component's orientation vector and a vector
starting at component's absolute position and ending at target. I.e.
how much the current component need to rotate to face the target. This
angle is measured in clockwise direction. target should be in
absolute/world coordinate system.

name: center
desc:
The position of the center of the component's bounding rectangle
in the parent's coordinates.

name: children
desc: The children components of this component.

name: componentsAtLocation
desc:
This is a generic implementation of componentsAtPoint; refer to those
docs for context.

name: componentsAtPoint
desc:
An iterable of descendant components intersecting the given point. The
point is in the local coordinate space.

name: contains
desc: Whether the children list contains the given component.

name: containsLocalPoint
desc:
Test whether the point (given in local coordinates) lies within this
component. The top and the left borders of the component are inclusive,
while the bottom and the right borders are exclusive.

name: containsPoint
desc:
Test whether the point (given in global coordinates) lies within this
component. The top and the left borders of the component are inclusive,
while the bottom and the right borders are exclusive.

the X and Y dimensions. A scale greater than 1 makes the component
bigger, and less than 1 smaller. The scale can also be negative,
which results in a mirror reflection along the corresponding axis.

name: SvgImage
desc:
Loads an SVG once and rasterizes it into a Sprite sized at the display
surface's physical-pixel resolution, then sizes the sprite in logical
pixels so it lands at the intended on-screen size. The DPR multiply keeps
icons crisp on high-DPI surfaces (e.g. Wear OS at DPR 2) without
over-rendering on DPR 1.

source before the initial rasterization and stored on the instance for
subsequent render calls.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: render
desc:
Re-rasterise the SVG. Invokes onRender (the argument here if given,
otherwise the instance field) with the original asset SVG string; the
result is rasterized at the same pixel scale used at load. The sprite's
image is swapped in place and the previous image is disposed.

name: load
desc:
Load an image from a path and cache it. If properties is supplied
without a name, the path will be used as the name. Additional
parameters can be provided to scale and resize the image. Only provide
properties or path, not both.

name: Tween
desc:
Handle to a tween scheduled on TweenService. The service owns the
instance; callers receive it from TweenService.add and use it to
inspect state, pause/resume, or cancel.

name: cancel
desc:
Stop the tween. Removes it from the service on the next sweep. Does
not fire onComplete. Safe to call from inside any callback.

name: delay
desc: Seconds to wait before onStart fires.

name: duration
desc: Total run time in seconds, excluding delay.

name: ease
desc:
Easing function applied to the normalised time t before scaling
between from and to.

name: from
desc: Start value passed to onUpdate when the tween begins.

name: hasStarted
desc: true once delay has elapsed and the run phase has begun.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: isActive
desc: true until the tween completes naturally or is cancelled.

name: isPaused
desc: true while paused by pause.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: onComplete
desc:
Fired once when the tween reaches to. Receives to. Not fired
when cancel is called.

name: onStart
desc:
Fired once when delay elapses, immediately before the first
onUpdate of the run. Receives from.

name: onUpdate
desc:
Fired on every update tick while the tween is running. Receives the
eased value scaled between from and to.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: pause
desc:
Suspend updates. The tween stops advancing until resume is called.
Has no effect once the tween has completed or been cancelled.

name: progress
desc:
Normalised progress in [0, 1] through the run phase (after delay).
Returns 0 while still in delay and 1 once complete.

name: resume
desc: Resume a paused tween. No-op if not currently paused.

name: runtimeType
desc: A representation of the runtime type of the object.

name: to
desc: End value passed to onUpdate on the final tick.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: TweenCallback
desc:
Callback for tween lifecycle events. Receives the current value:
from for onStart, the eased value for onUpdate, to for
onComplete.

name: TweenService
desc:
Drives a list of Tweens every frame. The default instance lives at
Services.tween and is ticked automatically by SizzleGame.update.

name: activeCount
desc:
Number of tweens currently being driven, including any added since
the last update call.

name: add
desc:
Schedule a new tween. Returns a Tween handle the caller can store
to query state, pause/resume, or cancel.

name: addColor
desc:
Schedule a ColorTween that interpolates between two Colors using
Color.lerp. Same lifecycle and timing semantics as add; the
callbacks receive colours instead of doubles.

name: cancelAll
desc:
Cancel every active tween. Does not fire onComplete for any of
them. Useful for tests and hard resets.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: runtimeType
desc: A representation of the runtime type of the object.

name: timeScale
desc:
Multiplier applied to dt on every update call. 0 freezes every
tween; values >1 fast-forward; the default 1.0 is real-time.

name: toString
desc: A string representation of this object.

name: update
desc:
Advance every active tween by dt seconds (scaled by timeScale).
Called by SizzleGame.update; callers should not invoke this
directly.

name: VariableWidthStroke
desc:
Generates a filled Path that outlines a stroke whose width varies along a
centreline (its "spine"). Filling this path — rather than stroking a line
with a fixed Paint.strokeWidth — is what lets the edge swell and taper the
way an ink-loaded nib does in calligraphy.

name: curve
desc:
Convenience wrapper around generateCurve that returns just the fillable
path: a variable-width stroke along the quadratic Bézier through start,
control and end.

name: generate
desc:
Generates a randomised variable-width stroke running straight from start
to end, returning the centreline, the two offset edges, and the outline.

name: generateCurve
desc:
Like generate, but the spine follows the quadratic Bézier curve defined
by start, control and end. Parameters and cap behaviour are identical
to generate; the wobble is applied perpendicular to the curve's local
tangent.

name: generatePath
desc:
Generates one continuous variable-width stroke along a multi-segment
StrokePath (a chain of lines and curves), returning the centreline, the
two offset edges, and the outline.

name: hashCode
desc: The hash code for this object.

name: line
desc: Convenience wrapper around generate that returns just the fillable path.

name: noSuchMethod
desc: Invoked when a nonexistent method or property is accessed.

name: operator ==
desc: The equality operator.

name: outline
desc: Builds a fillable outline around spine.

name: path
desc:
Convenience wrapper around generatePath that returns just the fillable
path.

name: boundingBox
desc: Calculate the bounding box of a rectangle rotated around its center.

name: buildAmountLut
desc:
Builds a 256x1 RGBA lookup image encoding radius-fraction-vs-position for
stops. The value is stored in the red channel and sampled by the shader
(which multiplies it by the max radius). The growth mapping is applied
here, so the shader stays identical regardless of mode.

name: pathDotsBakeBounds
desc:
The pixel-snapped output rectangle a whole-dot HalftoneRenderer.bake
(clip: false) uses when auto-fitting: path's bounds inflated by spill
(from HalftoneRenderer.pathDotsSpill) and rounded outward to whole pixels.
Its topLeft is the resulting HalftoneBake.origin and its size the image
dimensions.
